data.name: Settings_Flyout_HeaderAtBottom_Description
data.value: Render the flyout's title bar (Settings cluster and Undock button) at the bottom of the flyout instead of the top.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_RestoreUndockState_Description
data.value: When the app launches, restore the flyout's docked or undocked state from the previous session. When off, the flyout always opens docked.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_ShowUndockButton_Description
data.value: Show the undock button in the flyout. When off, the flyout always stays anchored to the tray.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_SoundSettingsTarget_Description
data.value: Which Windows surface the flyout's sound-settings titlebar button opens.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_DeviceLayout_Description
data.value: Where each device's row sits relative to its app sliders.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_DeviceTitlePosition_Description
data.value: Where the device name and per-device action buttons sit relative to the device slider.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_CommunicationsButtonVisibility_Description
data.value: Controls when the communications-activity glyph appears in the flyout header. "When ducking is on" hides it whenever Windows is set to "Do nothing" on the Communications tab.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_DeviceSort_Description
data.value: Default sort: default, default communications, enabled, disabled, disconnected. The default device sits at the bottom of the flyout. Windows sort: untouched MMDevice enumeration order.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_ShowRecordingDevices_Description
data.value: Include capture endpoints (microphones, line-in) in the flyout's device list. Still gated by the global recording-devices toggle on the Devices page.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_IntermixRecording_Description
data.value: When on, recording devices interleave with playback devices inside each sort bucket. When off, all playback devices show first and recording devices group together at the top of the list.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_ShowDeviceFormatText_Description
data.value: The compact format strip (sample rate, bit depth, channels) drawn under each device name. Right-clicking the strip opens the format picker. Off by default.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_ShowDeviceCodecText_Title
data.value: Show Bluetooth codec on the format strip

data.name: Settings_Flyout_ShowDeviceCodecText_Description
data.value: Suffix the format strip with the live A2DP codec (SBC, AAC, aptX, LDAC, etc.) on Bluetooth playback devices. Independent of the format readout toggle - with that off, the codec name renders alone for Bluetooth devices. Requires running the app elevated; otherwise the codec stays unknown. Off by default.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_RecordingAppDrawerDisplayType_Description
data.value: Displaying applications with volume mixer sliders for recording devices is pointless, as recording devices do not go through a volume mixing layer. But this is still here if you want to make the flyout look consistent.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_AppDrawerIconsCentered_Description
data.value: How the trailing partial row anchors. Off keeps it left-aligned; Centered shifts it to the middle; Centered soft-max left-anchors at the position a centered soft-max-icon row would occupy until the row exceeds soft-max, then crosses over to fully centered.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_AppDrawerIconsCenterSoftMax_Description
data.value: Anchors the growing row at the position a centered row of this many icons would occupy. Past this count the layout switches to fully centered.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_AppDrawerIconScale_Description
data.value: Scales the app icons in the grid drawer. 100 matches the slider-drawer baseline; values above 100 enlarge the icons while keeping eight per row.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_AppDrawerIconsPerRow_Description
data.value: Caps how many icons fit on a row in the grid drawer. Values above 8 shrink each cell to keep the grid inside the drawer; values below 8 leave the cells at their default size and the grid reads narrower.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_AppDrawerIconsPerColumn_Description
data.value: Caps how many icons fit in a column in the grid drawer. Once a column fills, the next icon starts a new column alongside it.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_AppDrawerStackDirection_Description
data.value: How the app icons stack inside the grid drawer. Auto picks the direction that puts the first app closest to the device row based on whether apps sit above or below it.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_AppDrawerMaxApps_Sliders_Title
data.value: Max apps before scrolling - Full sliders

data.name: Settings_Flyout_AppDrawerMaxApps_Sliders_Description
data.value: Caps how many app slider rows render before the drawer enters scrolling overflow.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_AppDrawerMaxApps_Icons_Title
data.value: Max rows before scrolling - Icon grid

data.name: Settings_Flyout_AppDrawerMaxApps_Icons_Description
data.value: Caps how many icon rows render before the drawer enters scrolling overflow.

data.name: Settings_General_InstallConfirm_Message_Format
data.value: This will copy the running exe to "{0}" and register it in Windows Settings > Apps.

data.name: Settings_General_InstallSystemWideConfirm_Message_Format
data.value: This will copy the running exe to "{0}" and register it in Windows Settings > Apps. Requires admin.

data.name: Settings_General_InstalledOutOfDate_Format
data.value: Installed build {0}, running build {1}. Click Update to overwrite.{2}

data.name: Settings_General_LogarithmicVolumeScale_Description
data.value: Map the slider to volume on a perceptual curve so equal slider deltas feel like equal loudness deltas.

data.name: Settings_General_NotInstalled_Format
data.value: Not installed. Will be placed at "{0}".{1}

data.name: Settings_General_PlayAppVolumeChangeSound_Description
data.value: Play a Windows notification sound when you change a per-app slider, scaled to the app's mixed volume so quieter apps preview quieter.

data.name: Settings_General_PlayAppVolumeChangeSound_Title
data.value: Play a sound when changing app volume

data.name: Settings_General_PlayDeviceVolumeChangeSound_Description
data.value: Play a beep through the playback device whose slider you adjust. Recording devices and per-app sliders stay silent.

data.name: Settings_General_PlayDeviceVolumeChangeSound_Title
data.value: Play a sound when changing playback device volume

data.name: Settings_General_RunOnStartup_Description
data.value: Launch VolumeTrayAppWPF when you sign in to Windows.

data.name: Settings_General_UninstallIncomplete_Message
data.value: Uninstall completed with warnings. Some files or registry entries may need manual removal. Check the install location and Windows Settings > Apps.

data.name: Settings_Hotkeys_SectionDescription
data.value: Bind a system-wide keyboard shortcut to each action. Bindings require at least one modifier key (Ctrl, Alt, Shift, or Win). All actions start unbound.

data.name: Settings_Theme_BackgroundColor_Description
data.value: Override the default background color. Each variant falls back to the default when unset.

data.name: Settings_Theme_FontSize_Description
data.value: Controls the overall size of the tray context menu. All elements scale from font size.

data.name: Settings_Theme_MeterPeakColor_Description
data.value: Color of the peak meter overlay drawn on top of the volume slider track.

data.name: Settings_Theme_MeterPeakStereoColor_Description
data.value: Color of the stereo overlay drawn on top of the base bar to the loudest channel. Translucent by default so the channel mismatch reads as a halo extending past the base.

data.name: Settings_Theme_MeterPeakFps_Description
data.value: How often the meter is redrawn. Higher than the sample rate buys smoothness via linear interpolation between samples; equal to the sample rate snaps with no interpolation.

data.name: Settings_Theme_MeterPeakSampleRate_Description
data.value: How often the raw audio peak is read from Windows. Lower than the refresh rate to enable smoothing; higher than the refresh rate wastes CPU.

data.name: Settings_Theme_MeterPeakChangeCeiling_Description
data.value: Maximum amount, in volume units (0 to 100), that the peak meter can move in a single redraw. Caps single-frame jumps so a sudden silence-to-loud transition ramps over a few frames. 0 freezes the meter; 100 disables the cap.

data.name: Settings_General_IconRetryInterval_Description
data.value: Wait between attempts when an app icon fails to resolve. Four attempts run with linear backoff (1x, 2x, 3x this value), so the worst case with the default is roughly 1.5 seconds before the fallback glyph stays.

data.name: Settings_General_CaptureActivityIndicator_Description
data.value: How the flyout marks apps that currently have the microphone open inside a recording device's drawer.

data.name: Settings_Theme_UnifiedPeakMeter_Description
data.value: Combine the left and right channels into a single weighted bar instead of drawing the stereo overlay on top of the base meter.

data.name: Settings_Theme_UnifiedMeterBias_Description
data.value: How strongly the quieter channel weights the combined value. 0 keeps the louder channel; 1 averages the channels; higher values dampen stereo flutter by biasing the result toward the quieter channel.

data.name: Settings_Theme_UnifiedMeterBias_Title
data.value: Unified meter low channel bias multiplier

data.name: Settings_Theme_RoundedCorners_Description
data.value: Applies rounded corners to the flyout, settings cards, buttons, and menus. Turn off for a squared-off look.

data.name: Settings_Theme_SliderIndicator_Description
data.value: The shape drawn as the volume-slider thumb. Each option keeps its own font and proportions.

data.name: Settings_Theme_TextColor_Description
data.value: Override the default text color. Each variant falls back to the default when unset.

data.name: Settings_Theme_ThemeStyle_Description
data.value: Follow Windows, or force light / dark mode for this app.

data.name: Settings_TrayIcon_DeviceNameMaxLength_Description
data.value: Names longer than this character count are truncated with a 2-dot ellipsis suffix.

data.name: Settings_TrayIcon_MenuPosition_Description
data.value: Classic opens at the cursor. Modern docks the menu above the taskbar like the Windows 11 system flyouts.

data.name: Settings_TrayIcon_ModifiedActions_Description
data.value: Assign actions to modified clicks on the tray icon. Entries set to Nothing fall back to the default behavior.

data.name: Settings_TrayIcon_MouseWheel_Description
data.value: Scroll the mouse wheel over the tray icon to adjust the default device's volume. Does *not* use an always-active global mouse hook.

data.name: Settings_TrayIcon_PlaybackDeviceName_Description
data.value: Which slice of each playback device's name appears in the tray context menu.

data.name: Settings_TrayIcon_RecordingDeviceName_Description
data.value: Which slice of each recording device's name appears in the tray context menu.

data.name: Flyout_DeviceIcon_Tooltip
data.value: Click: set as default. Ctrl+click: enable / disable. Shift+click: set as default communications.

data.name: Flyout_ListenButton_Tooltip
data.value: Click: toggle Listen to this device. Ctrl+click: enable with Default Playback target. Right-click: pick target device.

data.name: Flyout_ExclusiveMode_Tooltip_Allowed
data.value: Exclusive mode is allowed. Click to disallow apps from taking exclusive control of this device.

data.name: Flyout_ExclusiveMode_Tooltip_Held
data.value: An app is currently holding this device in exclusive mode. Click to disallow exclusive control.

data.name: Flyout_ExclusiveMode_Tooltip_Disallowed
data.value: Exclusive mode is disallowed. Click to allow apps to take exclusive control of this device.

data.name: Flyout_EqualizerAPO_Tooltip_Running
data.value: Equalizer APO is running on this device. Click to uninstall. Ctrl+click or right-click: open the Configuration Editor.

data.name: Flyout_EqualizerAPO_Tooltip_EnhancementsOff
data.value: Equalizer APO is installed but enhancements are disabled for this device. Click to enable. Ctrl+click or right-click: open the Configuration Editor.

data.name: Flyout_EqualizerAPO_Tooltip_NotInstalled
data.value: Equalizer APO is not installed on this device. Click to install. Ctrl+click or right-click: open the Configuration Editor.

data.name: Flyout_EqualizerAPO_Tooltip_NotAvailable
data.value: Equalizer APO is not detected on this system. Click for installation options.

data.name: EqualizerAPO_NotAvailable_Body
data.value: Equalizer APO was not found on this system. Set its install location in the app settings, or download the latest x64 installer.

data.name: Settings_Devices_SetDefaultCommsToDefault_Title
data.value: Tie default comms device to default device

data.name: Settings_Devices_SetDefaultCommsToDefault_Description
data.value: When you set a default device through this app, also promote it to the communications-role default. Only triggered by your own clicks; never reacts to other apps' default-changes.

data.name: Settings_Devices_ShowDisabledPlayback_Description
data.value: List playback devices that are disabled in Windows. Off by default.

data.name: Settings_Devices_ShowDefaultPlaybackEvenIfDisabled_Title
data.value: Show default playback device even if disabled

data.name: Settings_Devices_ShowDefaultPlaybackEvenIfDisabled_Description
data.value: Always include the default playback device in lists, even when it's disabled in Windows.

data.name: Settings_Devices_ShowDefaultCommsPlaybackEvenIfDisabled_Title
data.value: Show default comms playback device even if disabled

data.name: Settings_Devices_ShowDefaultCommsPlaybackEvenIfDisabled_Description
data.value: Always include the communications-role default playback device, even when it's disabled in Windows.

data.name: Settings_Devices_ShowDisconnectedPlayback_Description
data.value: List playback devices Windows reports as currently unplugged but still recognized. Registry-only ghost endpoints have their own setting below.

data.name: Settings_Devices_ShowRecording_Description
data.value: Master switch for recording-device entries. When off, every recording-side option below is ignored.

data.name: Settings_Devices_ShowDisabledRecording_Description
data.value: List recording devices that are disabled in Windows.

data.name: Settings_Devices_ShowDefaultRecordingEvenIfDisabled_Title
data.value: Show default recording device even if disabled

data.name: Settings_Devices_ShowDefaultRecordingEvenIfDisabled_Description
data.value: Always include the default recording device, even when it's disabled in Windows.

data.name: Settings_Devices_ShowDefaultCommsRecordingEvenIfDisabled_Title
data.value: Show default comms recording device even if disabled

data.name: Settings_Devices_ShowDefaultCommsRecordingEvenIfDisabled_Description
data.value: Always include the communications-role default recording device, even when it's disabled in Windows.

data.name: Settings_Devices_ShowNotPresent_Description
data.value: List endpoints Windows still has on file but no driver is currently loaded for - old USB DACs, removed GPUs' HDMI outputs, paired Bluetooth headsets. Most surface as "Unknown Device". Off by default.

data.name: Settings_Devices_ShowPlaybackLockButton_Description
data.value: The lock / unlock glyph that toggles the endpoint's "Allow applications to take exclusive control" bit.

data.name: Settings_Devices_ShowPlaybackEqualizerAPOButton_Description
data.value: The equalizer glyph that reflects Equalizer APO state and toggles install / enable on click.

data.name: Settings_Devices_ShowPlaybackDefaultDeviceButton_Description
data.value: The device icon button on the right edge of the row. Left-click sets default, right-click opens the device menu.

data.name: Settings_Devices_ShowRecordingLockButton_Description
data.value: The lock / unlock glyph that toggles the endpoint's "Allow applications to take exclusive control" bit.

data.name: Settings_Devices_ShowRecordingEqualizerAPOButton_Description
data.value: The equalizer glyph that reflects Equalizer APO state and toggles install / enable on click.

data.name: Settings_Devices_ShowRecordingListenButton_Description
data.value: Adds a button to each recording row that toggles the Windows "Listen to this device" feature. Left-click toggles, Ctrl+left-click enables with Default Playback as the target, right-click picks a specific target.

data.name: Settings_Devices_ShowRecordingDefaultDeviceButton_Description
data.value: The device icon button on the right edge of the row. Left-click sets default, right-click opens the device menu.

data.name: Settings_Devices_ShowTrayRecordingLink_Description
data.value: Add an entry that opens the classic Sound control panel on the Recording tab.

data.name: Settings_Devices_ShowTraySoundsLink_Description
data.value: Add an entry that opens the classic Sound control panel on the Sounds tab.

data.name: Settings_Devices_ShowTrayCommunicationsLink_Description
data.value: Add an entry that opens the classic Sound control panel on the Communications tab.

data.name: Settings_Devices_ShowTrayDeviceLinks_Title
data.value: Show per-device links in tray menu

data.name: Settings_Devices_ShowTrayDeviceLinks_Description
data.value: Add a sub-menu entry per visible enabled device. Each entry opens the matching classic Sound tab (same as Ctrl+click on the device icon in the flyout).

data.name: Uninstaller_DefaultDescription
data.value: This will remove the installed copy of VolumeTrayAppWPF and its registration in Windows Apps. Choose what to do with your settings.

data.name: Uninstaller_DeleteSettings_DefaultDescription
data.value: Also remove the VolumeTrayAppWPF folder under %LOCALAPPDATA%, including settings.xml.

data.name: Uninstaller_Description_Format
data.value: This will remove VolumeTrayAppWPF installed at "{0}" and its entry in Windows Settings > Apps. Choose what to do with your settings.

data.name: Uninstaller_KeepSettings_Description
data.value: Leave settings.xml in place so a future install picks them up.

data.name: Settings_General_StoreRunning
data.value: Running from the Microsoft Store install.

data.name: Settings_General_StoreNotInstalled
data.value: Not installed from the Microsoft Store.

data.name: Settings_Hotkeys_OpenFlyout_Description
data.value: Show the volume flyout near the tray icon.

data.name: Settings_Theme_StaticIconColor_Description
data.value: Override the tray icon color when Tray icon style is set to Static. Each variant falls back to the default when unset.

data.name: Settings_Theme_StaticIconColor_LightTooltip
data.value: Light theme static tray icon color

data.name: Settings_Theme_StaticIconColor_DarkTooltip
data.value: Dark theme static tray icon color
